Title: Maternal Smoking and Low Birthweight by Maternal Race — Hosmer & Lemeshow 1989
Source: Hosmer, D.W. and Lemeshow, S. (1989). Applied Logistic Regression. Wiley. R MASS::birthwt dataset (Baystate Medical Center, Springfield MA, 1986).
note: Maternal smoking is associated with higher low-birthweight rates in both White and NonWhite strata (0.365 vs 0.091 for White; 0.500 vs 0.352 for NonWhite). The pooled direction is the same (smokers: 40.5% vs non-smokers: 25.2%). Race stratification amplifies the smoking effect in White mothers but does not reverse the pooled finding.

treatments.Description: Mother did not smoke during pregnancy

strata.Description: Black or Other maternal race (race=2 or 3)
